rationale: The cited 2015 paper is highly topically and methodologically aligned with the citing 2018 paper (both use fMRI multivoxel/decoding approaches to recover brand associations from brain activity). The citing note explicitly says it extends prior brand-based decoding work that assumes predefined brand attributes, which matches the cited paper’s model-based brand-personality decoding contribution, so this is plausibly foundational/method-relevant rather than an incidental mention.
